raw_text:
```json
{
  "claim_texts": [
    "The California State Legislature has passed more than 20 bills to address the fentanyl and overdose crisis.",
    "The State Assembly’s Fentanyl, Opioid Addiction, and Overdose Prevention Committee is the first committee in the nation focused specifically on combating the fentanyl crisis.",
    "The committee convenes leaders from various sectors, including opioid addiction experts and medical professionals, to address fentanyl deaths.",
    "AB 663 allows medicine for opioid addiction to be dispensed by mobile pharmacies to increase treatment access for vulnerable communities.",
    "AB 816 permits 16 and 17-year-olds, unable to obtain parental consent, to receive treatment for opioid addiction.",
    "AB 461 expands access to fentanyl test strips on California’s college campuses.",
    "SB 10 requires schools to have emergency protocols for youth experiencing an opioid overdose.",
    "AB 641 allows the State to distribute stronger doses of overdose reversal medicine to communities in crisis.",
    "AB 1060 mandates that insurance companies cover overdose reversal medications in their plans.",
    "SB 234 requires amusement parks, stadiums, and concert venues to carry overdose reversal medications.",
    "AB 701 increases penalties and fines for trafficking large amounts of fentanyl.",
    "SB 46 requires individuals with drug offenses to complete a treatment or education program while on probation.",
    "SB 19 establishes a first-ever Anti-Fentanyl Task Force in the state of California."
  ]
}
```
